question: Will CPI rise more than 0.9% in March 2026?
reasoning: The March 2026 CPI data has already been officially released by the Bureau of Labor Statistics on April 10, 2026. The Consumer Price Index for All Urban Consumers (CPI-U) increased only 0.3% in March on a seasonally adjusted basis, which is significantly below the 0.9% threshold required for a YES resolution. This makes the probability of YES resolution exactly 0% since the data is already known.
key_factors: Official BLS March 2026 CPI report shows 0.3% monthly increase
Data released on April 10, 2026 at 8:30 AM ET
Market asking if CPI rises more than 0.9% - actual result was 0.3%
No uncertainty remains as official data is public
risks: Potential data revision by BLS within 5-year revision period
Market resolution may depend on specific decimal precision interpretation
